title: Atomic Design Rules
description: Per-repo configuration consumed by the make-no-mistakes atomic-design hooks (PreToolUse: ownership enforcement; PostToolUse: drift telemetry). Drop this file at consumer repo root as .atomic-design-rules.json. If absent, the atomic-design hooks are a no-op.

version.description: Schema version. Current schema is version 1.

components_root.description: Path to the root of the components tree, repo-relative (e.g. 'src/components'). Used to detect pillar boundaries and atomic-level subfolders.

pillars.description: Authoritative pillar list. Each pillar names a subfolder under components_root and the owner responsible for it. Cross-pillar imports must go through the platform pillar (or shared).

slug.description: Kebab-case identifier used in error messages and Linear refs (e.g. 'chimera-score').

folder.description: Folder name under components_root (e.g. 'chimera-score'). Must be the CANONICAL form — see canonical_folders for plural/singular disambiguation.

owner.description: Owner identifier (Slack handle, Linear team, or person). Surfaced in violation messages so reviewers know who to ping.

max_organisms.description: PostToolUse drift threshold — warn when this pillar exceeds N organisms. Default 100 if omitted.

shared_pillars.description: Pillars that are allowed to be imported from any other pillar (cross-pillar imports). Typically ['platform', 'shared', 'ui'].

atomic_levels.description: Atomic-design taxonomy. Each level lists the folder name and the constraints that apply when writing into it. The 'atoms' level enforces statelessness per Brad Frost 2013 + atomic-methodology.md.

atoms.forbid_message: Atoms must be stateless. Move stateful logic to a molecule or organism.

canonical_folders.description: Plural/singular canonical-form pairs. For each entry, the 'wrong' form is blocked and the user is pointed at 'canonical'. Helps prevent the 'courses/' vs 'course/' drift documented in legacy-ticket.

wrong.description: Path segment (relative to components_root) that is NOT canonical. Block writes here.

canonical.description: Path segment that IS canonical. Show this in the error message.

reason.description: Short rationale shown in the error (e.g. 'singular per legacy-ticket').

junk_drawers.description: Folders known to accumulate misplaced files. Writes to these paths are blocked unless they specify a deeper sub-pillar. Each entry can require a sub-folder to be specified.

folder.description: Junk-drawer path (e.g. 'src/components/dashboard'). Writes to FILE.tsx directly under this folder are blocked; writes to sub-folders are allowed.

message.description: Custom message shown to the user explaining which pillar the file likely belongs in.

drift_thresholds.description: PostToolUse drift thresholds. The post-hook scans the filesystem and emits warnings when exceeded — never blocks.

max_root_files_per_pillar.description: Max files directly at the root of a pillar folder (must use atoms/molecules/organisms/templates subdirs). Default 5.

public_prefix_stale_days.description: Warn when a file with the Public* prefix has existed > N days without consolidation (per the canonical-URL principle from legacy-ticket). Default 30.

exempt_markers.description: In-file comment markers that exempt a write from atomic-design enforcement. Default '@atomic-exempt'. The hook scans for '// @atomic-exempt: <reason>' or '# @atomic-exempt: <reason>' in the content payload.

rules_doc_url.description: Optional URL to the consumer's rules documentation (e.g. PROJECTS_BIBLE.md or legacy-ticket spike). Surfaced in violation messages so the user can read the full rationale.

folder.description: Folder name for this atomic level (e.g. 'atoms', 'molecules').

forbid_content_patterns.description: ERE regex patterns. If ANY pattern matches the content of a file being written to this atomic level, the write is blocked.

forbid_message.description: Custom message shown when a forbidden pattern matches.
forbid_message.default: Content violates atomic-design constraints for this level.
